The Lotus plant (Nelumbo nucifera) is a type of aquatic plant that has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The health benefits of the Lotus plant include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce pain, improve wound healing, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of the Lotus plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with the Lotus plant include teas, infusions, and capsules, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
